text.title: MCP Transport Decision — Three Options, One Rule
text.summary: Pick stdio for local subprocess servers, Streamable HTTP for remote/multi-tenant, never SSE in greenfield code.

section.title: The decision rule

rule.statement: For every new MCP server, pick exactly one transport from {stdio, streamable-http}. Choose stdio iff the server runs as a subprocess of a single client on the same machine. Choose streamable-http iff the server is reached over a network OR serves more than one concurrent client. Treat SSE as deprecated — only enable it behind a `legacy_sse: true` feature flag with a documented sunset date.
rule.rationale: MCP spec revision `2025-11-25` removed the dual-endpoint SSE transport in favor of a single Streamable HTTP endpoint that can negotiate POST → SSE upgrade per request. stdio remains the only zero-overhead option for local IPC; HTTP is the only option that scales horizontally. SSE is transitional and slated for removal.

section.reference: Bright Data — SSE vs Streamable HTTP comparison.

list.item: stdio: Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Cursor plugins, local CLI tools, fast launch, single-user.
streamable-http: hosted SaaS MCP servers, multi-tenant, behind LB, OAuth 2.1, resumable streams.
SSE: legacy clients pinned to pre-`2026-03-26` protocol — ship only as a back-compat shim.

example.code: # Local stdio — one process per Claude Desktop session from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP mcp = FastMCP("github-local") mcp.run(transport="stdio")
example.note: Subprocess of the client; lifecycle tied to parent; no network surface.

example.code: # Remote streamable HTTP — single endpoint /mcp behind nginx + OAuth 2.1 mcp.run(transport="streamable-http", host="0.0.0.0", port=8080)
example.note: Horizontally scalable; supports POST + SSE upgrade per request; resumable.

example.code: # 2026 greenfield with SSE transport mcp.run(transport="sse", host="0.0.0.0", port=8080)
example.note: Deprecated transport; clients are migrating off it; you will rewrite this within months.

section.title: Forbidden mixes

antipattern.description: Running stdio in production multi-user deployment ("we will spawn one stdio process per request").
antipattern.reason: Process-per-request defeats connection reuse, breaks OAuth flows, and saturates fork()/spawn() under load. Use HTTP.

antipattern.description: Greenfield server that supports SSE "for compatibility" without naming a single client that needs it.
antipattern.reason: SSE is transitional. Without a named legacy client and a sunset date, you are paying maintenance tax on dead code.

section.reference: One-screen decision flow for design docs.
